title: ADMP Exceptions and Exemptions
description: Exceptions disapply Right to Refuse and Right to Human Review (but not Right to Information). Exemptions disapply all ADM Restrictions. Source: JPDP ADMP Guideline (PCP No. 3/2025 substantive content).

exceptions.scope: Exceptions disapply the Right to Refuse and Right to Human Review. The Right to Information is RETAINED — transparency obligations remain.

list.name: Contract necessity
list.rule: Where the processing is necessary for entering into, or performance of, a contract between the data subject and a data controller.

list.name: Legal compliance
list.rule: Where the processing is necessary for compliance with laws.

list.name: Explicit consent
list.rule: Where the data subject has given prior explicit consent.
list.explicitConsentRequirement: Consent must be clear, specific, and require a direct action from the data subject (e.g., checking a tick box, signing a document).

exceptions.sensitiveDataAdditionalRule: Automated decision-making involving sensitive personal data is only allowed where (a) explicit consent OR processing necessary for public interest; AND (b) suitable safeguards in place.

exemptions.scope: Exemptions disapply ALL ADM Restrictions (including the Right to Information). Based on Section 45 of Act 709.

list.name: Personal/family/household
list.rule: Personal data processed for individual's personal, family or household affairs, including recreational purposes.

list.name: Crime prevention/detection/prosecution
list.rule: Personal data processed for: (i) prevention or detection of crime or for the purpose of investigations; (ii) apprehension or prosecution of offenders; or (iii) the assessment or collection of any tax or duty or any other imposition of a similar nature.

list.name: Statistics or research
list.rule: Personal data processed for preparing statistics or carrying out research, provided that such data is not processed for any other purpose and the resulting statistics/research are not made available in a form which identifies the data subject.

list.name: Court order or judgment
list.rule: Personal data necessary for the purpose of or in connection with any order or judgment of a court.

list.name: Regulatory functions
list.rule: Personal data processed for the purpose of discharging regulatory functions.

list.name: Journalism, literature, art
list.rule: Processed only for journalistic, literary or artistic purposes, provided that: (i) processing is undertaken with a view to publication; (ii) the data controller reasonably believes publication would be in the public interest considering freedom of expression; and (iii) the data controller reasonably believes compliance with the relevant provision is incompatible with the journalistic/literary/artistic purpose.

decisionLogic: Step 1: Does an Exemption apply (Section 45-derived)? → If YES: no ADM Restrictions apply.
Step 2: Does an Exception apply (contract / legal compliance / explicit consent)? → If YES: Right to Refuse and Right to Human Review do not apply BUT Right to Information still applies.
Step 3: Otherwise: all three ADM Restrictions apply.
Step 4: For sensitive personal data: even with an Exception, processing is only allowed under explicit consent or public interest, with suitable safeguards.
Step 5: For children: profiling and ADM for advertising/marketing is prohibited unless demonstrably in the child's best interests — see dpbd/childrens-privacy.json.
